behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: manifest parser + dependency graph resolver + scanner input assembly. A unit test of only parsing misses transitive-resolution wiring bugs. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is about extraction/inclusion, not real HTTP/OS/subprocess or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Use real manifest/lockfile fixtures on local filesystem; run extraction pipeline end-to-end in-process; assert full direct+transitive set is passed to vulnerability-check module.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: This behavior targets real query semantics at an external vulnerability API boundary; a stub can trivially return version-correct payloads and hide real request/contract mismatches. Integration was rejected because the behavior explicitly says the database query returns matching results, which depends on real HTTP/API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real (or production-faithful live) vulnerability API endpoint, submit dependencies with controlled version ranges and assert returned CVEs correspond to those ranges, not package-name-only matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Pure deterministic ordering logic inside one function/object boundary; no cross-module or I/O dependency is required to expose a real failure. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text being only ranking rules.
behaviors.test_description: Call sorter with unsorted CVE fixtures spanning severities and equal-severity CVSS ties; assert exact sorted order.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears across parser/edit/serializer modules: write-back may unintentionally alter unrelated entries. Unit-testing only version-selection logic misses serialization side effects. System rejected because local file/module wiring is sufficient; no external OS/network/subprocess boundary is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update pipeline on representative real manifest files, then structural-diff before/after and assert exactly one dependency version changed and all other keys/values are unchanged.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires creation on a real repository, so real VCS/HTTP API/subprocess interactions are the failure boundary. Mocks can hide auth/permission/branch/diff publication failures. Higher agentic/workflow levels are disqualified because no AI decision quality or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, execute PR creation flow end-to-end (branch, commit, push, PR API) and assert PR exists with a one-dependency-only diff.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic; one function boundary is enough to catch real failures. Higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is strictly comparison logic, not module wiring or infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate semver comparison function with cases like 1.x->2.x, 0.x edge cases, prereleases, and equal versions; assert breaking-change classification.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff between semver classifier, PR-creation gate, and report generator. Unit tests on any single function miss cross-module control-flow bugs. System rejected because behavior is about skip/flag logic and can be validated with in-process collaborators; no real external call is required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire semver detection, PR service interface, and reporter in one test; feed a breaking update; assert PR creation path is not invoked and review flag appears in final scanner output.
